title: NLF vs Cheeks: How to decide when cheeks are needed too
filename: NLF vs Cheeks： How to decide when cheeks are needed too.en.vtt
excerpt: So they will actually say i really don't like my nasolabial fold while they pull their cheeks up how do you know when actually the cheeks are that's just a symptom and the cause is here well you always start by looking at the whole shape of their head and you're looking you i loo…

segments.text: so they will actually say i really don't like my nasolabial fold while they pull their cheeks up how do you know when actually the cheeks are that's just a symptom and the cause is here well you always start by looking at the whole shape of their head and you're looking you i look for the volume in their cheek but you can also feel it so you can actually just put your hand on and feel and sometimes i'll show my patients this can you feel how much less fat there is here compared with here and that's that the loss of the lateral fat pad and you can also see it when they move so if you ask your patient to smile as they talked about before you can see the junction and between the anterior fat pad and where it's depleted laterally and that little junction here if you reconnect that is more beautiful so it's kind of look see feel and ask them to move and then you immediately get into the problem i've had this loads of times on the phones when they've presented for nasolabials and you know the real problem is here as we know cheeks take more so you're immediately into a price battle aren't you because now you're trying to argue for something

that won't give such an immediate imp you know such a fabulous oh yeah it's all gone but we'll make them holistically look better but it's gonna take four syringes instead of potentially just one so that like with a lot of great consultations it's about how good you are teaching your patients so um and what you're in is so one of the things i like to do is i look for the non-verbal communication when they're assessing themselves when they're trying to explain the problem they often show you the problem without meaning to so they will actually say i really don't like my nails leave your fold while they pull their cheeks up and i would reference that and say did you notice when you explained it to me what you did and that's actually because intuitively you understand that the physiology of aging is meaning that your cheek fat is falling down and that's why you have a fold so we need to lift it up and that is linked with what they say if you ignore what they say and you start giving advice it sounds like a sales trick so if you say no you don't need nasolabial fold you need your cheeks but you haven't explained why and this does happen all the time the patient automatically and probably
rightfully not rightfully but it's reasonable that they think this but this is just you trying to maximize your sale value so you need to explain things very clearly
